greeting: Well hello there, fellow pixel wranglers and code conjurers!
introParagraph: This week's 'Release Radar' is pinging with a delightful mix of performance polish and nifty new features. While some plugins were busy patching up the digital equivalent of leaky pipes, others rolled out the red carpet for exciting new functionalities, making your WordPress life just a little bit smoother and more powerful. Let's dive into the bits and bytes!

highlights.title: WPForms Lite 1.10.1.1
highlights.summary: WPForms just made payment integration a breeze by adding inline Connect buttons directly in the Form Builder's Payments panel, alongside a sleek, refreshed design for the Payments page. Now, connecting your payment accounts is smoother than ever, without ever leaving the form editor.

highlights.title: PushEngage 4.2.5
highlights.summary: PushEngage is empowering teams with new role-based post-editor access, letting non-admin users utilize the PushEngage metabox. Plus, their audience filters got a significant upgrade with date-based operators for attribute predicates, bringing parity with the App Dashboard.

highlights.title: All in One SEO 4.9.7.2
highlights.summary: AIOSEO squashed a rather critical bug, fixing a race condition that could corrupt the cache table on some sites after the 4.9.7 update. This fix ensures your cache writes don't silently overwrite unrelated entries, keeping your SEO data pristine and performant.

highlights.title: Uncanny Automator 7.3.1.1
highlights.summary: Uncanny Automator ironed out a tricky 'Non-JSON response from API' error that could occur with app actions when vendors sent a 204 No Content status. This fix ensures your automations run smoothly, even when APIs are being a little too minimalist with their responses.

closingJoke: Why did the WordPress plugin break up with the theme? It felt like they just weren't compatible anymore, and the theme was always trying to style its output!
